title: Employee Business Expense Deduction Reinstatement Act of 2025
short_title: Employee Business Expense Deduction Reinstatement Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill lets employees get money back on their taxes for work expenses their company did not pay for. Workers can deduct eighty five percent of costs like meals, travel, and transportation they paid for their job. This tax break will last until two thousand twenty seven. People can also ask for refunds on taxes they already paid in past years when these deductions were not allowed.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Ways and Means.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Employee Business Expense Deduction Reinstatement Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Unreimbursed Employee Expenses Taken Into Account As Miscellaneous Itemized Deduction
sections.text: This section changes the tax code to help employees who pay for work expenses out of their own pocket. Right now, workers cannot deduct these expenses on their taxes through two thousand twenty seven. This law creates an exception for certain work expenses. Starting now, employees can deduct eighty five percent of money they spend on food, hotels, travel, or transportation for work when their employer does not pay them back. To qualify for this deduction, these expenses must be more than one percent of the person's income instead of the usual two percent. These changes apply as if they were part of the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act that passed in two thousand seventeen. If someone already filed their taxes and paid too much because they could not take this deduction, they have one year from when this law passes to ask for their money back, even if the normal time limit has passed.
